suspected_functions.reason: Frame #0 crash point. The function dereferences rrcSetupComplete->dedicatedNAS_Message.buf without a NULL check on rrcSetupComplete itself, leading to a potential NULL pointer dereference and segmentation fault.

Frame #1 immediate caller. This function calls rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ and is responsible for passing the rrcSetupComplete parameter. It lacks validation of the rrcSetupComplete pointer before passing it, which may cause the crash.

Upstream caller of rrc_gNB_decode_dcch. It handles incoming messages and dispatches them. If it passes malformed or incomplete messages leading to invalid rrcSetupComplete pointers, it contributes to the crash.

Calls rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ multiple times, including once with a NULL rrcSetupComplete pointer (commented as removed to prevent crash). Indicates potential misuse or improper NULL handling of rrcSetupComplete parameter.

fix_suggestion.reason: The segmentation fault occurs because rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ is called with a NULL pointer for rrcSetupComplete in rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete. The function uses rrcSetupComplete without null checks, causing a crash. The root cause is improper handling of the rrcSetupComplete pointer, especially the second call with NULL after setting it to NULL explicitly. Also, the function lacks proper error handling to reject the UE connection gracefully when rrcSetupComplete is invalid. The missing else branch in conditional logic and missing calls to rrc_gNB_generate_RRCReject in error paths cause the crash.

code_patches.description: From commit c2e2561fbe03d6e48b11fb680ccf5230059d8436: Fix NGAP protocol issue
From commit c2e2561fbe03d6e48b11fb680ccf5230059d8436: Fix NGAP protocol issue
From commit c2e2561fbe03d6e48b11fb680ccf5230059d8436: Fix NGAP protocol issue

fix_suggestion.root_cause_analysis: The segmentation fault is caused by a NULL pointer dereference in rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ when it is called with a NULL rrcSetupComplete pointer. This happens because rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete calls rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ twice: once correctly with a valid pointer, and once incorrectly with NULL after setting rrcSetupComplete to NULL explicitly. The function rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ does not check for NULL before dereferencing, causing the crash. Additionally, the code lacks proper error handling to reject the UE connection gracefully when invalid or unexpected input is received. Missing else branches in conditional logic handling UE identity or RRC setup requests cause uninitialized pointers to be used, leading to crashes. Proper initialization of UE context and calling rrc_gNB_generate_RRCReject in error paths is mandatory to prevent segmentation faults and ensure protocol compliance.
investigation_steps: Review rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete to identify calls to rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ and check parameter validity.
Add null pointer checks in rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ to prevent dereferencing NULL pointers.
Audit all if-else if chains handling UE identity or RRC setup requests for missing else branches and add them to handle unexpected cases.
Ensure that in error handling paths, UE context pointers are properly initialized before calling rejection functions like rrc_gNB_generate_RRCReject.
Test with malformed or unexpected RRCSetupComplete messages to verify graceful rejection and no crashes.
Verify that no code calls rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ with NULL parameters after patching.
fix_suggestion.specification_context:
3GPP SPECIFICATION REFERENCE
================================================================================

**Section: 11.2 Inter-node RRC messages (Pages 851, 861, 863)**
Specifies RRC messages transferred between network nodes (gNB, AMF, etc.) via X2, Xn, or NG interfaces.
RRC messages use a single logical channel for inter-node communication, with the same transfer syntax and protocol extensions as NR radio interface messages.
Messages may originate from or be destined for another RAT, implying careful handling of message encoding/decoding and protocol state.
Relevant for `rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ` as this function sends initial NAS messages over NG interface, requiring correct message construction and UE context validation.
Includes ASN.1 definitions and message structures critical for encoding/decoding RRC and NGAP messages, which if malformed or null can cause segmentation faults.
(Pages 851, 861, 863; Section 11.1 and 11.2)
**Section: NGAP and NAS message handling (implied from error context)**
The function `rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ` likely constructs and sends the NGAP Initial UE Message containing NAS payload after RRC Setup Complete.
Proper UE context pointer validation is mandatory before accessing UE data structures to avoid null pointer dereference.
NAS message encoding must follow 3GPP TS 38.413 and TS 24.501 specifications to ensure correct message format.
Inter-node signaling procedures require synchronization of UE state between RRC and NGAP layers; failure to do so can cause crashes.
(Inferred from 3GPP TS 38.413 and TS 24.501, related to NGAP and NAS signaling)
**Section: RRC Connection Establishment and NAS Initial Message Procedures (general 3GPP context)**
After RRC Setup Complete, the gNB sends an Initial UE Message to AMF including NAS Initial UE message.
This procedure involves:
Valid UE context allocation and validation.
Correct construction of NGAP Initial UE Message with NAS payload.
Handling of optional parameters and critical extensions as per 3GPP TS
[Content truncated for display]

Test basic network connectivity to the AMF IP to ensure signaling path is reachable
Verify routing to AMF IP to confirm packets are correctly routed through the network

crash_detailed_fixes.error_text: SIGSEGV - Segmentation fault in rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ at /home/tcs/jayasvi/nov_2025_dev/ue/openairinterface5g/openair2/RRC/NR/rrc_gNB_NGAP.c:241 (Thread: TASK_RRC_GNB)

fix_suggestion.reason: The crash is caused by dereferencing the rrcSetupComplete pointer without checking if it is NULL. Additionally, in rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete, rrcSetupComplete is explicitly set to NULL and then passed to rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ, causing a NULL pointer dereference. Also, rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ does not check if the allocated message pointer is NULL before use.

code_patches.description: Remove the assignment of NULL to rrcSetupComplete and the subsequent call with NULL parameter to prevent passing NULL pointer causing crash
Add NULL check for rrcSetupComplete pointer and check for successful allocation of message_p before use to prevent NULL dereference
Add NULL check for decoded UL-DCCH message pointer before use to prevent dereferencing NULL pointer

fix_suggestion.root_cause_analysis: The segmentation fault occurs because rrcSetupComplete pointer is passed as NULL to rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ, which dereferences it without checking for NULL. The function rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete explicitly sets rrcSetupComplete to NULL and then calls the crash function, causing the fault. Additionally, rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ does not check if the message allocation succeeded before using the pointer. Adding NULL checks and removing invalid NULL assignments prevents the crash.
investigation_steps: Step 1: Search all suspected functions for NULL assignment patterns and found rrcSetupComplete=NULL in rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete.
Step 2: Identify that rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete sets rrcSetupComplete to NULL and calls the crash function, causing NULL dereference.
Step 3: Add defensive NULL checks in rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ and rrc_gNB_decode_dcch to prevent dereferencing NULL pointers.
fix_suggestion.specification_context:
3GPP SPECIFICATION REFERENCE
================================================================================

**Section: 11.2 Inter-node RRC messages (Page 851, 861, 863)**
RRC messages transferred between network nodes (gNB, ng-eNB, CU, DU) use a single logical channel across X2, Xn, or NG interfaces.
Messages may originate from or be destined for another RAT; transfer syntax and protocol extension mechanisms are consistent with NR radio interface.
gNB can request secondary gNB or eNB to establish, modify, or release SCG; similarly, CU can request DU to perform actions on MCG or SCG.
Critical for `rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ` as this function sends initial NAS messages over NG interface, implying correct message construction and inter-node signaling adherence.
ASN.1 definitions and message structures (e.g., MeasurementTimingConfiguration, UERadioPagingInformation) provide templates for message encoding/decoding, crucial to avoid segmentation faults due to malformed messages or null pointers.
**Section: MeasurementTimingConfiguration and Related ASN.1 Structures (Page 861, 863)**
MeasurementTimingConfiguration message includes nested CHOICE and SEQUENCE types with optional fields.
Proper handling of optional fields (e.g., measTiming, nonCriticalExtension) is essential to prevent null dereferences.
Fields like `campOnFirstSSB`, `psCellOnlyOnFirstSSB`, and `csi-RS-CellMobility` require valid initialization before encoding.
Ensuring all mandatory fields are set and optional fields are checked before access can prevent SIGSEGV in message assembly functions like `rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ`.
**Section: General RRC Message Transfer and Inter-node Signaling (Page 851, 861)**
RRC messages sent over NG interface must conform to defined transfer syntax and protocol extensions.
Logical channel usage and message direction (gNB to 5GC or gNB to gNB) must be respected.
Proper memory allocation and pointer validation before message encoding and sending are implied by the specification.
This context highlights the importa

commit.subject: Fix NGAP protocol issue
commit.author: chinchans <vangalachandu77@gmail.com>
commit.body:
Fix NGAP protocol issue

Error Message: "Segmentation Fault in rrc_gNB_send_NGAP_NAS_FIRST_REQ at /home/tcs/jayasvi/cu/openairinterface5g/openair2/RRC/NR/rrc_gNB_NGAP.c:173"

Applied 3 code patches and 0 config patches.

Selected Patch Details:
- Code: rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete (rrc_gNB.c) (rrc_gNB.c)
Description: Remove the rrcSetupComplete=NULL statement that was setting parameter to NULL before use
- Code: rrc_gNB_process_RRCSetupComplete (rrc_gNB.c) (rrc_gNB.c)
Description: Remove the rrcSetupComplete=NULL statement that was setting parameter to NULL before use
